meta_title: For Students: content creation to Solve can't debug code (Intermediate Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to content creation and overcome can't debug code. A Intermediate-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Debug Code via Content Creation: A Student's Guide
content.seo_description: Master debugging by creating technical content. Learn to use Antigravity and Claude Code to document your build process and ship faster as a developer student.
content.answer_block: Transform debugging from a chore into a content engine. By documenting your roadblocks, you force yourself to articulate the logic, which often reveals the error. Leverage Antigravity to structure your technical documentation and use Claude Code to automate code analysis, turning every bug into a high-value piece of technical content.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a terminal full of red text, the silence of your room amplified by the flickering cursor. You've hit that dreaded wall—the one where logic feels like a foreign language and your project feels like a sinking ship. Every developer has been here, but the best ones don't just fix the code; they deconstruct the failure.

What if you could turn that frustration into your greatest career asset? By pivoting your workflow toward content creation, you stop treating bugs as failures and start treating them as case studies. This shift doesn't just fix your app—it builds your reputation as an engineer who understands the 'why' behind the code.

steps.title: Isolate the Logic Failure
steps.description: Before fixing, you must articulate the bug. Write a concise explanation of the expected vs. actual behavior. This forces cognitive clarity.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the following code block for logical inconsistencies and explain the failure point in plain English for a technical blog post.
steps.claude_code_command: claude read ./src/logic.ts --ask 'Identify the specific logical flaw in this function.'

steps.title: Draft the 'Why' Documentation
steps.description: Create a 'bug-log' entry. This acts as a draft for a future technical article while helping you trace the execution flow.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a structured outline for a 'How I Solved This Bug' tutorial based on the current error stack.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write ./docs/debugging_log.md --content 'Describe the bug, the hypothesis, and the failed attempts.'

steps.title: Automated Code Analysis
steps.description: Use Claude Code to perform a deep-dive audit of the failing module to identify edge cases you missed.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review this module for edge cases that cause runtime errors. Provide a list of 3 potential fixes.

steps.title: Refactor for Readability
steps.description: Clean code is easier to debug. Refactor your code as if it were being published in an open-source repository.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor this code to follow clean architecture principles, making it readable for a tutorial audience.

steps.title: Publish and Validate
steps.description: Finalize your technical post by incorporating the solution. This reinforces your learning and adds to your portfolio.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a compelling LinkedIn post summary based on the technical solution we just implemented.
steps.claude_code_command: claude commit -m 'Fixed bug and documented logic for portfolio'

pro_tips: Treat every terminal error as a prompt for a 'Lesson Learned' blog post to improve your technical writing.
Use Claude Code to generate unit tests for every bug you fix to prevent regression.
Keep a 'Bug-to-Feature' journal; often, the hardest bugs lead to the most unique feature ideas.
Always explain your code in comments as if you are teaching a junior peer; it highlights your gaps.
Use Antigravity to simulate user behavior to find bugs before they even happen in production.
content.next_steps: Take the documentation you created today and publish it on your developer blog or GitHub Wiki to solidify your authority and build your portfolio.
content.conversion_block: You are no longer a student struggling with errors; you are a builder documenting the evolution of your software. Take control of your debugging process and start shipping code that matters. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
